response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong craftsmanship, TDD, and distributed-systems experience, but the role is specifically a C++ senior position requiring deep C++/STL, multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity expertise. C++ exposure is limited to a short ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no evidence of recent C++ work, industrial automation, or SDL/IEC 62443 experience. Stronger fit for a TypeScript/Node full-stack role than this C++ industrial position.
strengths: 10+ years of senior engineering with strong TDD, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship discipline
Distributed/event-driven systems experience (microservices, low-latency, message-driven backends in DLang)
Mentoring and technical communication proven via workshops, talks, and courses
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old — no recent hands-on C++/STL work
No industrial automation, MQTT, Modbus/OPC UA, or IEC 62443 / SDL background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack (Node, TypeScript, DLang), not systems-level C++ for on-premises industrial platforms

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / SDL experience
Strong engineering fundamentals and distributed-systems background
